channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: March megastorm may bring blizzards, tornadoes, flooding and even fires across much of US
item.description: More than 100 million people in the U.S. will be in the path of an intense March storm starting Friday as the sprawling multi-day system threatens fires, blizzards, tornadoes, and flooding as it tracks eastward across the Great Plains.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Farmers sue Trump administration over halted IRA grants
item.description: Farmers and environmental groups are suing the Trump administration over its decision to pause grants that are part of the Democrats’ climate, tax and healthcare law.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man describes cruelty during his two decades of captivity at his family home in Connecticut
item.description: A man rescued from a burning home in Connecticut told a shocking story of cruelty and constant hunger as he was held captive in a single room for 20 years by his father and stepmother, according to a newly released arrest warrant.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Universal Orlando opens ticket sales for Epic Universe, set to debut in May
item.description: Universal Orlando Resort has opened ticket sales for its newest theme park, Universal Epic Universe, ahead of its scheduled grand opening on May 22, 2025. Starting today, visitors can purchase general admission tickets, including single-day and multi-day options, for visits beginning in June 2025.
item.dc:creator: Brody Wooddell, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump pushes annexation of Greenland during meeting with NATO secretary
item.description: President Trump on Thursday expressed confidence the United States would annex Greenland, even suggesting the head of the NATO alliance could be a key player in facilitating the acquisition.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Judge orders Trump to rehire probationary workers let go in mass firings across multiple agencies
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge in San Francisco ordered President Donald Trump's administration to rehire thousands, if not tens of thousands, of probationary workers let go in mass firings across multiple agencies, blasting their tactics Thursday as he slowed the new president's dramatic down
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Oklahoma lawmakers push to eradicate invasive red cedar trees amid fire danger
item.description: "The oils and the resins that are in the trees make them just nearly explosive in the right conditions," said Rep. Dobrinski.
item.dc:creator: Sydnee Batzlaff/KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: Third MLB hat pulled over accidental vulgarity as others sell for hundreds online
item.description: Yet another newly released MLB hat has been pulled from online stores after fans noted the potentially crass design the cap sported.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kidnapped man tries to get message to store employees: Police
item.description: The victim told police that the man had tied him up at gunpoint, driven him around until daylight, and made him withdraw $6,000.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Moon, NewsNation

item.title: Students protest Tennessee bill that could deny education for undocumented kids
item.description: "This gives an option to the local districts to require that proof of citizenship just as they require proof of residency or immunization," the sponsor of bill said.
item.dc:creator: Kendall Ashman, NewsNation

item.title: Starbucks announces big changes to in-store experience
item.description: Starbucks is rolling out new ideas to make its stores more inviting to customers and recapture some of the "vibe" from its early days.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Pete Buttigieg won't seek US Senate seat in Michigan, leaving door wide open for 2028 run
item.description: SAUGATUCK, Mich. (AP) — Former Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg announced that he will not seek an open U.S. Senate seat in Michigan, a move that leaves the door wide open for him to seek a much bigger role as his party’s next presidential nominee.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pete Buttigieg declines Senate run in Michigan
item.description: Former Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg announced he will not run for Senate in Michigan, keeping the door open for a potential presidential run in 2028.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, WGN 9

item.title: Missing American in Dominican Republic: No suspects as search for Sudiksha Konanki reaches one week
item.description: Dominican authorities have expanded the search for missing Sudiksha Konanki, a UPitt student from Virginia who vanished from the Riu Republica resort a week ago on spring break.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democratic-led states sue to block Trump administration layoffs at the Education Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A coalition of Democratic-led states is challenging the Trump administration’s sweeping layoffs across the Education Department, saying it amounts to an illegal dismantling of an agency created by Congress.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FAFSA site goes down after mass layoff at Education Department
item.description: The Studentaid.gov website was down for hours on Wednesday after the Education Department laid off half its workforce.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dem AGs sue over Trump administration’s Education Department layoffs
item.description: Democratic attorneys general in Washington, D.C., and 20 states sued Thursday over the Trump administration’s efforts to lay off nearly half of the Education Department workforce.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: US' foreign-born, immigrant populations reach record highs: report
item.description: The number and share of the U.S. population that is foreign-born or are immigrants reached record highs in January 2025, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump threatens Europe with 200 percent tariff on wine, Champagne
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday threatened to put a hefty 200 percent tariff on wine and Champagne coming from Europe if the European Union (EU) does not remove a tariff on whisky.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: 5 risks facing the US economy under Trump
item.description: Warning signs have been flashing and markets have been sliding amid policy uncertainties and some troubling macroeconomic indicators.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, KRON4

item.title: Columbia grad student's detention will stretch on as lawyers spar over Trump's plan to deport him
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Mahmoud Khalil will remain detained in Louisiana until at least next week but can finally speak to lawyers while they fight the Trump administration’s plans to deport the Columbia University graduate student for his role in campus protests against Israel, a judge decided at a hearing
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks parts of Trump's executive order seeking to punish law firm Perkins Coie
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge blocked President Donald Trump's administration Wednesday from enforcing portions of an executive order designed to punish a prominent law firm linked to Democratic-funded opposition research during the 2016 presidential campaign into ties between the Republican can
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tijuana airport blockage enters third day
item.description: Residents claim AMLO gave them full ownership and title to their land, something they now say is being "stolen" by state and local officials.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration resumes detention of immigrant families after Biden-era pause
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — The Trump administration resumed family detention of immigrants last week in a South Texas facility after a Biden-era pause, a legal nonprofit group providing services to migrant families said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Conn. woman held stepson captive for over 20 years: Police
item.description: While being evaluated by EMS for smoke inhalation, the victim allegedly disclosed to first responders that he intentionally set the fire in his upstairs room because he "wanted his freedom.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Lepore, NewsNation

item.title: Passenger attacked flight attendant, swallowed rosary beads on plane to Miami: court docs
item.description: His sister told the FBI agent that they were traveling to Haiti “to flee religious attacks of a spiritual nature.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Schools use AI to monitor kids, hoping to prevent violence. Our investigation found security risks
item.description: One student asked a search engine, “Why does my boyfriend hit me?” Another threatened suicide in an email to an unrequited love. A gay teen opened up in an online diary about struggles with homophobic parents, writing they just wanted to be themselves.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE BRYAN of The Seattle Times and SHARON LURYE of The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Democrat David Gottfried easily wins Minnesota House special election, restoring a 67-67 power split
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Voters in a Minnesota House district at the center of post-election drama over chamber control decisively chose Democrat David Gottfried on Tuesday, tying the House and ending a short-lived Republican majority.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lone Democrat to back House GOP spending bill explains decision
item.description: Rep. Jared Golden (D-Maine) defied House Democratic leadership Tuesday when he voted for the GOP’s bill to avert a government shutdown — the only one in the caucus to support the stopgap. Golden’s “yes” vote was not the decisive one, since Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WGN 9

item.title: A federal judge has halted Louisiana’s first nitrogen gas execution. The state says it will appeal
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — A federal judge has halted Louisiana’s first death row execution using nitrogen gas, which was scheduled to take place next week. U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Department of Education lays off nearly half of workforce
item.description: The Department of Education announced Tuesday it was firing nearly half of its workforce, the latest Trump administration move to shrink the federal government that could face swift legal challenge.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New lawsuit claims Girl Scout Cookies contain 'heavy metals' and pesticides
item.description: A new federal lawsuit claims Girl Scout Cookies contain heavy metals, but the organization assures the cookies are safe.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: Why you'll no longer see 'excessive heat warnings' in the forecast
item.description: It may be hard to think about sweltering temperatures in early March but excessive heat watches and warnings are getting a new name.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wall Street ends manic day modestly lower after briefly dropping more than 10% below its record
item.description: The U.S. stock market fell further Tuesday following President Donald Trump’s latest escalation in his trade war, briefly pulling Wall Street 10% below its record set last month. And like it's been most of the past few weeks, the market’s slide on Tuesday was erratic and dizzying.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's approval dips amid concerns over economy: Poll
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating has dropped a few points since he first took office, with voters increasingly expressing concerns over the state of the economy, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: 15 injured, 1 seriously, when school bus overturns in New Jersey: officials
item.description: At least 14 juveniles were injured when a school bus overturned on the Garden State Parkway in New Jersey on Monday, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Bags fly free no more: Southwest Airlines to end free checked luggage policy
item.description: Southwest Airlines will no longer allow bags to fly free. The airline announced Tuesday it will begin charging some customers who check baggage in May.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Adams, ABC 4
